items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: Whose 1967 Treatise is a famous graphic score?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cornelius Cardew. “John Cage” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was MIDI standardised?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1983. “1979” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Around what year did Les Paul pioneer multitrack recording?
items.why: Exact answer: 1947 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
